criteria_results.title: Allegation 1: Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations â€” Identified
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output has a dedicated section titled 'Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations' that clearly identifies this as a distinct allegation category. It describes the pattern of disproportionately allocating profitable trades to Oceanic Fund I LP (with excess profits of $7,800,000) and Chao's personal account (81.6% profitable trades, $438,000 in excess profits), along with the harm caused to other clients. The allegation is thoroughly described with specific figures and details.

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: Correct Statutory Provisions Cited
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly cites Sections 206(1) and 206(2) of the Advisers Act in connection with cherry-picking trade allocations. In the 'Conflict of Interest' section, it states: 'Section 206(1) (15 U.S.C. § 80b-6(1)) and Section 206(2) (15 U.S.C. § 80b-6(2)) are alleged to have been violated through cherry-picking trade allocations, misleading Form ADV disclosures, and breaches of fiduciary duty.' This directly satisfies the criterion.

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: WCA Identified as Implicated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output clearly identifies WCA (Whitmore Capital Advisors LLC) as an implicated party in the cherry-picking allegation. In the 'Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations' section, the output explicitly states 'the firm engaged in cherry-picking practices by allocating favorable trades to the Oceanic Fund I LP' and references WCA throughout as the entity responsible for the cherry-picking scheme. WCA is also identified as implicated across all six allegation categories in multiple sections of the output.

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: Whitmore Identified as Implicated (12% LP Interest)
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly identifies Whitmore's 12% limited partnership interest in Oceanic Fund I LP in the 'Item 10 - Other Financial Industry Activities and Affiliations' section, stating 'As of December 8, 2023, Whitmore holds a 12% limited partnership interest in Oceanic Fund I LP, representing approximately $22.2 million in value as of December 31, 2023. Superior investment performance in this fund directly increases the value of Whitmore's limited partnership interest.' The output also connects this interest to the cherry-picking allegations by noting that Whitmore's financial interest in Oceanic Fund I LP created an incentive to favor that fund in allocation decisions, implicating him in the cherry-picking scheme.

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: Chao Identified as Implicated (Personal Account)
